w:r.w:t: Initial creation of the Software Design Document. This version defines the overall system architecture, design components, and technical structure of the XchangeSkills Platform.
This Software Design Document presents the detailed design of the
. The purpose of this document is to describe how the requirements specified in the Software Requirements Specification (SRS) are translated into a concrete system design prior to implementation.

The document explains the overall system architecture, data design, and key components involved in the platform. It provides a technical blueprint that outlines how different parts of the system interact to support functionalities such as user management, skill listing, service requests, booking management, chat communication, and reviews.
, ensuring that the system design is developed after requirement finalization and before implementation. While the SRS defines
the system should do from a user perspective, this document focuses on
the system will internally manage data, interactions, and control mechanisms to ensure reliability, scalability, and fairness.
The XchangeSkills platform is designed using a
is used for frontend development and
is used as a headless backend that exposes functionality through RESTful APIs. This design approach supports modular development, ease of maintenance, and future scalability.

Overall, this document serves as a reference for developers, evaluators, and stakeholders to understand the internal design decisions of the system and to ensure that the final implementation remains consistent with the approved requirements.
Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) (To be developed using Microsoft Visio or any other drawing software of your choice)
The Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) represents the logical data model of the

. It identifies the main entities involved in the system, their attributes, and the relationships between them. The ERD provides a clear understanding of how data is structured and how different entities are linked to support the systemâ€™s core functionalities.
The ERD has been designed with a focus on
. Separate entities are defined for users, roles, profiles, skills, categories, booking requests, chat sessions, messages, and reviews to avoid redundancy and to support efficient data management.

The diagram illustrates how users can offer skills, request services, participate in chats, and provide reviews after service completion. It also reflects the systemâ€™s ability to manage service requests with defined scope, time, duration, and location, ensuring proper coordination between service providers and requesters.
Microsoft Visio or an equivalent diagramming tool
, and it serves as the foundation for database design and implementation in later phases of the project.
Sequence Diagrams (To be developed using Rational Rose or any other drawing software of your choice)

The system provides visual notification indicators to inform users of important events such as booking approvals, incoming messages, or status updates. These notifications serve as usability enhancements and do not constitute standalone system functionality.
TC-01.01: Successful registration with valid data
TC-01.02: Email validation - duplicate email rejected
TC-02.01: Successful login with valid credentials
TC-03.01: Send password reset email successfully
TC-04.01: Change password with correct current password
TC-05.01: Create skill with all required fields
TC-07.01: Delete skill and verify removal
TC-07.02: Cannot delete skill with active requests
TC-08.01: Load and display skills marketplace
TC-09.01: Search and filter skills by name
TC-10.01: Successfully send booking request with valid data
TC-11.01: Load and display all requests
TC-11.02: Filter requests by status (pending, approved, rejected)
TC-12.02: Cannot approve already approved request
TC-13.01: Reject request with reason provided
TC-13.02: Cannot reject already rejected request
TC-14.01: Create chat session after request approval
TC-14.02: Cannot chat with blocked user
TC-20.02: Unblock user and restore access
TC-21.01: Report user with reason and description
TC-22.01: Update profile information (bio, phone, location)
TC-22.02: Upload and change profile picture
Precondition: Admin on category management page
Expected Result: Category created, appears in list
Precondition: Category exists with no skills
TC-23.06: Cannot Delete Category With Skills
Expected Result: Error "Cannot delete category with active skills"
Expected Result: All categories displayed with skill count
Expected Result: Filtered results showing matching categories
Precondition: Non-admin tries to manage categories
Precondition: Admin on role management page
Select permissions: view reports, approve skills
Expected Result: Role created with permissions
Precondition: Role exists with no users assigned
Expected Result: Error "Cannot delete system role"
TC-24.07: Cannot Delete Role With Users
Expected Result: Error "Cannot delete role with assigned users"
Expected Result: User role updated, permissions applied
Expected Result: All assigned permissions displayed
